title: Ephemerantha xantholeuca
intro: Ephemerantha xantholeuca, also known as the yellow leuca orchid, is a species of orchid native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Ephemerantha xantholeuca include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, often used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as skin conditions, such as eczema and acne.
